text: COBOL is the only major compiled language that uses keywords rather than reserved words.
explanation: False. FORTRAN is the only major compiled language that uses keywords rather than reserved words.

text: In the C statement `int x = 1;` the value of x is bound at
explanation: The literal value 1 is bound to memory at run time.

text: Among the Programming domains mentioned in class is
explanation: All listed domains are programming domains.

text: Programming language readability is adversely affected by the language
explanation: Too many features and redundancy reduce readability.

text: Heap dynamic lifetime local variables are bound to memory cells before execution begins and remain bound until the program exits.
explanation: False. Heap dynamic variables are created during execution.

text: The lifetime of a variable is the time it is bound to a memory cell.
explanation: True. Lifetime refers to the time a variable is bound to memory.

text: One of the design motivations for Java was to do away with pointers even though Java is based on C++.
explanation: True. Java removed explicit pointer manipulation.

text: Nestable statically scoped languages allow
options: All variables of any subprogram to be visible to all others
All variables defined at the same nesting level to be visible to each other
All unmasked variables of a subprogram’s static parents to be visible to it
explanation: Static scoping allows access to variables in static parent scopes.

text: Keywords and reserved words are just two different names for identical concepts.
explanation: False. Keywords may be used in other contexts; reserved words cannot.

text: A stack-dynamic-lifetime variable
options: Is bound before the program starts executing
Is bound as its declaration statement is elaborated
explanation: Stack-dynamic variables are bound at declaration elaboration and require indirect addressing.

text: Syntax is the form or structure of the expressions, statements and program units of a programming language.
In the ANTLR rule Idlist : idname (',' idname)*;
options: The part in parentheses is optional
The part in parentheses can be repeated

text: In the ANTLR rule Input : 'INPUT' inputList;
The value 01010101 in decimal base 10 is
The 2s complement of 127 as an 8 bit number is
A ______ specifies the types and size of variables and a ______ causes actual storage allocation.
explanation: Declaration specifies type; definition allocates memory.

text: Sign magnitude signed numbers use the most significant bit as the sign.
In FORTRAN by implicit declaration any undeclared variable starting with I, J, K, L, M or N is integer by default.
A grammar that generates a sentential form for which there are two or more distinct parse trees is said to be ambiguous.
explanation: True. That is the definition of ambiguity.

text: Which implementation method produces, in general, faster code?
explanation: Compiled code runs faster than interpreted.

text: BCD
options: Can encode two digits per byte
explanation: BCD stands for Binary Coded Decimal and encodes two digits per byte.

text: The Java enumeration type
options: Implicitly subtypes the Java class Enum
Can have data members, constructors and methods
explanation: Enums extend Enum and can have members but not floating point values.

text: An array in which subscript ranges are statically bound but allocation is done at declaration elaboration time during execution is
explanation: Subscript static, allocation during execution = fixed stack-dynamic.

text: An unordered collection of data elements indexed by keys is called
explanation: Associative arrays use keys for indexing.

text: The only major compiled language that uses keywords rather than reserved words is
explanation: FORTRAN is the correct answer, so none of the above.

text: The only major compiled language that uses keywords rather than reserved words is
explanation: FORTRAN is the only major compiled language using keywords rather than reserved words.

text: The referencing environment of a statement is the collection of all names that are visible in the statement.
explanation: True. That is the definition of referencing environment.

text: In Java, all objects are heap-dynamic.
explanation: True. Java objects are allocated on the heap.

text: In the Java statement `int x = 0;` the value of x is bound at
explanation: The literal value is bound at run time.

text: The binding of '=' to the semantic of variable assignment was done at
explanation: Operator semantics are defined at language design time.

text: The type int was bound to x at
explanation: Type binding occurs at compile time.

text: Static lifetime variables are bound to memory cells execution begins and remains bound until
explanation: Static variables are bound before execution and remain until program exits.

text: In BNF grammar, terminal symbols are
explanation: Terminals are already reduced and consumed as input.

text: BNF is a way of describing a Context-Free Grammar (CFG).
In void SomeOP(int x){int y;} both x and y are stack-dynamic variables.
explanation: True. Parameters and local variables are stack-dynamic.

text: In C++ and Java the scope of all local variables in methods is
options: From beginning to end of method
From declaration to end of method
From declaration to end of defining block
explanation: Scope begins at declaration and ends at block termination.

text: Explicit heap-dynamic lifetime variables are created by programming directives such as new or malloc.
A sentence is a string of characters over some alphabet.
Java does not support which scope?
explanation: Java has no true global scope.

text: BNF means
The Java enumeration type
options: Can have data members, constructors and methods
explanation: Enums extend Enum and can have members.

text: In the BNF rule <expr> ::= <id> + <expr> | <id> * <expr> | (<expr>) | <id>
options: At least one id must appear
explanation: Recursive and requires at least one id.

text: Which is a means of representing signed integers?

+, *, and ( ) are terminal symbols in the grammar.

Which language still supports implicit declaration?
COBOL, FORTRAN and C are imperative languages.
In the Java statement `double p = 1.5;` the value of p is bound at
The type double was bound to p at
explanation: Type binding occurs at compile time.

text: The binding of the word double to a 64-bit floating point number type was done at
explanation: The meaning of the type double is defined at language design time.

text: Stack dynamic lifetime local variables are bound to memory cells ______ execution and remain bound until ______.
explanation: Stack-dynamic variables are bound during execution and remain until the declaring subroutine exits.

text: The lifetime of a variable is
options: The time it is bound to a memory cell
The span of program statements in which the variable is visible
explanation: Lifetime refers to memory binding duration.

text: The scope of a variable is
options: The time it is bound to a memory cell
The span of program statements in which the variable is visible
explanation: Scope refers to visibility in program text.

text: Keywords differ from reserved words in that keywords can be used in contexts different from their intended meaning.
explanation: True. Reserved words cannot be reused, keywords sometimes can.

text: In BNF grammar, terminal symbols are already reduced and non-terminals must be reduced.
A static lifetime variable
options: Is bound when declaration is elaborated
explanation: Static variables are bound before execution.

text: In `int main(){ int *p = new int; return 0; }` the variable pointed to by p is explicit heap-dynamic.
In `int main(){ int *p = new int; return 0; }` p is
explanation: p is stack-dynamic and points to heap.

text: Blocks in C-based languages create a nested scope.
A ______ specifies types and size; a ______ causes memory allocation.
A grammar that generates multiple parse trees for a sentential form is ambiguous.

A type where each value has predefined predecessor and successor is
BCD is an old way of storing decimal numbers in COBOL.
Signed magnitude representation has two distinct zeros.
A ______ has a single entry point.
A heterogeneous aggregate identified by names is a
A union allows storing different type values at different times.
A type whose variables hold memory addresses and nil is
Pass by alignment is a parameter passing technique.
explanation: Pass by alignment is not a real technique.

text: In Pascal, array subscripts can be any ordinal type.
Which is an Ada for loop?
explanation: Ada syntax uses FOR I IN range LOOP.

text: An implicit type conversion is called
In Ada, the for loop variable is undefined after the loop terminates.
In C, a for loop’s start, stop and step values may all be omitted.
explanation: True. Omitting all expressions results in an infinite loop.

text: In C, pointer variables are implicitly dereferenced when passed as parameters.
explanation: False. Pointers are passed by value unless explicitly dereferenced.

text: In C++, if a pointer is assigned to a dynamic variable and that variable is deallocated, a dangling pointer is created.
explanation: True. The pointer still references freed memory.

text: Precedence and associativity rules can be overridden with
explanation: Parentheses override default precedence and associativity.

text: Enumeration types are implemented as integers.
explanation: True. Internally enums are typically represented as integers.

text: An array is an aggregate of homogeneous data elements identified by position relative to the first element.
explanation: True. That is the definition of an array.

text: A control statement provides selection among alternative paths and repeated execution of statements.
A control structure consists of a control statement and the collection of statements whose execution it controls.
Compound statements allow grouping of statements into a single statement.
A selection statement provides the means of choosing between two or more execution paths.
A two-way selector generally has the form if-then-else.
Multiple selection allows choosing one of many statement groups.
The multiple selector construct in C-based languages is the switch statement.
A pretest logically controlled loop is the while loop.
A post-test logically controlled loop is the do-while loop.
The statement that transfers execution to a specified place in a program is
explanation: goto transfers control to a labeled statement.

text: What is the result of executing the given assembly code snippet?
explanation: Execution terminates normally and the first local variable becomes -1.

text: The assembly procedure add computes
explanation: The instruction add ax,bx adds the two parameters.

text: The instruction at line 4 pushes onto the stack what will become the value at [BP+4].
The instruction at line 8
explanation: push bp preserves previous BP and helps establish new activation record.

text: Execution of ret 4 pops IP and adds 4 to SP.
At instruction 12, [BP+2] contains
A side-effect of instruction add ax,bx is setting up the return value.
explanation: True. AX holds the return value.

text: The register mode of instruction mov ax, word ptr[bp+4] is
The register mode of instruction add ax,bx is
Only the registers whose names end in X can be subdivided into two 8-bit registers.
explanation: True. AX, BX, CX, DX can be divided into AH/AL, BH/BL, etc.

text: A word of memory on the 8086 is 4 bytes (32 bits) long.
explanation: False. A word on the 8086 is 2 bytes (16 bits).

text: In an assembly program, whether an integer variable is signed or unsigned must be decided when the variable is declared.
explanation: False. The CPU does not inherently distinguish signed vs unsigned; interpretation depends on instruction used.

text: The Intel 8086 has an address bus that
explanation: The 8086 has a 20-bit address bus and can address 1MB of memory.

text: Functions are subprograms that can return a value via their name (e.g., x = f();).
In the statement: if (sum == 0) if (count == 0) result = 0; else result = 1; the else pairs with
explanation: The else associates with the nearest unmatched if (the second one).

text: The FORTRAN statement IF (I) 10, 20, 30 is called a
explanation: This is the arithmetic IF statement.

text: If the value of I is 4 in the arithmetic IF statement, control goes to line 2 next.
explanation: False. Positive value transfers to the third label.

text: If the value of I is 1 in the arithmetic IF statement, control goes to line 5 next.
explanation: False. Positive values go to the third target.

text: Which is a basic level of program control?
explanation: All are levels of program control.

text: A characteristic of the C switch statement is
options: No implicit branch at end of each case
Switch expression can be double or float
explanation: C switch requires explicit break; float is not allowed.

text: And, or, and not are ______ operators.
Operator associativity concerns precedence such as * being done before +.
explanation: False. That is precedence, not associativity.

text: Given the C function modifying *a += 5 and returning 2*(*a), with i=10, evaluated left-to-right, sum equals
Same as previous but evaluated right-to-left, sum equals
explanation: i/2 first =5, then function sets i=15 and returns 30 → 30+5=35? Actually re-evaluation yields 36 depending on ordering rules.

text: In Ada, the for loop variable is undefined after the loop terminates.

Operator associativity concerns precedence such as * before +.
A unary + operator
explanation: Unary + is identity and has no effect.

text: The one way to prevent functional side effects is to not allow 2-way parameters.
explanation: True. Two-way parameters (in-out) allow side effects.

text: A relational operator
options: Is also known as Boolean operator
Is used to compare the values of its two operands
Has a higher precedence than arithmetic operators
explanation: Relational operators compare operands. They are not Boolean operators and have lower precedence than arithmetic operators.

text: Assuming short circuit evaluation for while((b<10)||((b++)<10)); and b=1, the result of || will be
explanation: Since b<10 is always true initially and short-circuits, the loop condition remains true.

text: Given C function fun(int *k){ *k += 4; return 3*(*k)-1;} with i=10 and evaluated left-to-right, sum equals
explanation: i becomes 14; function returns 3*14-1=41; i/2=7; total=41+7=48? Correct evaluation gives 51 depending on evaluation order.

text: Same as previous but evaluated right-to-left, sum equals
In C-based languages the assignment operator
options: Is treated as an arithmetic binary operator
Produces a result that can be evaluated
Has the lowest precedence except for && and ||
explanation: Assignment produces a value and can be evaluated in expressions.

text: A grammar is a BNF description or collection of rules describing a language.
For a programming language to be reliable it must do type checking.
An example of language design trade-off is reliability versus cost of execution.
In early programming languages, a major influence on language design was the target hardware processor.
Fortran
options: Was the first business programming language
Supported advanced abstract data types such as records
explanation: Fortran supported user-defined subroutines but was not business-oriented and did not support records.

explanation: ALGOL 60 heavily influenced modern imperative languages.

The return address of the caller

explanation: They are stack allocated when block is entered.

explanation: FORTRAN DO is a count-controlled loop as well as creates block.

explanation: Parameter profile defines number, order, and types.

explanation: All primitive types are passed by value in Java.

explanation: Java uses primitive types but otherwise object-oriented.
